rulings.Case Title: UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. JENNICA M SHERWOOD
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment, And To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, May 20, 2019, line 1. DEFENDANT JENNICA SHERWOOD'S Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment, And To Quash Service Of Summons. GRANT. No opposition filed. The parties stipulated to the relief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats the tentative verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: KRISTINA NENAYDOKH VS. GREG SHENKMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Order Finding Judgment Debtor In Contempt Of Court And For Monetary And Non-Monetary Sanctions Against Him
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 20, 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFF KRISTINA NENAYDOKH's Motion For An Order Finding Judgment Debtor In Contempt Of Court And For Monetary And Non-Monetary Sanctions Against Him Pro Tem Judge Douglas Robbins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Judgment Creditor Kristina Nenaydokh ("Nenaydokh") Motion for Order Finding Judgment Debtor Greg Shenkman ("Shenkman") in Contempt of Court and for Sanctions is GRANTED IN PART AS UNOPPOSED and DENIED IN PART. Nenaydokh shall prepare a form of the order recapitulating this tentative ruling and shall bring a copy of the proposed order to the hearing, if any. In the absence of a hearing, the prevailing party shall timely comply with Cal. R. Ct. 3.1312. [ENTIRE TENTATIVE RULING HAVE BEEN SENT DIRECTLY VIA EMAIL] Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to drobbins@woodrobbins.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BRETT TODOROFF VS. JOE QUINN BRETZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendant Randolph Bretz For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, May 20, 2019, line 2. DEFENDANT RANDOLPH BRETZ'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. OFF CALENDAR. "Before filing a motion for judgment on the pleadings pursuant to this chapter, the moving party shall meet and confer in person or by telephone¿" (CCP § 439 (emphasis added).) The declaration of David Nemecek fails to show that the parties met and conferred in compliance with the statute. The court will not entertain defendants' alternative common law motion for judgment on the pleadings as a means to circumvent the meet and confer requirement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: FELISIA THIBODEAUX VS. BAYVIEW HUNTERS POINT MULTIPURPOSE SENIOR SERVICES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendants Bayciew Hunters Point Multipurpose Senior Services And Cathy Davis Notice And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, May 20, 2019, line 6. DEFENDANTS CATHY DAVIS, BAYVIEW HUNTERS POINT MULTIPURPOSE SENIOR SERVICES'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendants Bayview Hunters Point Multipurpose Senior Services and Cathy Davis' motion for summary judgment is CONTINUED to May 31, 2019. Defendants' separate statement references the Declaration of Cathy Davis with attached resignation letter and letter accepting the resignation. However, the Davis Declaration was not included with Defendants' moving papers. Furthermore, Plaintiff Felisia Thibodeaux fails to attach to the attorney declaration all of the excerpts of deposition testimony on which she relies to defend against this motion. Both parties are instructed to file their supporting evidence by 5:00 p.m. on May 22, 2019 to give the Court the opportunity to review it in advance of the hearing. Plaintiff is urged to attach the entirety of the deposition testimony rather than excerpts in order to avoid further omissions of evidence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: R. MARTIN ROSCHEISEN ET AL VS. DEVLIN/MCNALLEY CONSTRUCTION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Contest The Good Faith Settlement Between Plaintiffs And Liberty Fire Systems, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, May 20, 2019, line 7. DEFENDANT DEVLIN/MCNALLY CONSTRUCTION, INC'S Motion To Contest The Good Faith Settlement Between Plaintiffs And Liberty Fire Systems, Inc. Defendants Devlin/McNally Construction, Inc., Davis Michael Devlin and Joseph McNally's motion contesting the good faith of the settlement between plaintiffs and Liberty Fire Systems, Inc. is GRANTED. The declaration filed with the good-faith application contains seemingly contradictory evidence regarding the settlement's amount. The application may be refiled with a clarifying declaration. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Devlin/McNally is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: CROWN BUILDING MAINTENANCE, INC. VS. METRO SERVICES GROUP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Response From Defendant Metro (Ccp Section 2030.300) And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Metro (Ccp Sections 2030.300, 2023.030)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, May 20, 2019, line 8. PLAINTIFF CROWN BUILDING MAINTENANCE, INC.'S Motion To Compel Further Response From Defendant Metro (Ccp Section 2030.300) And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Metro (Ccp Sections 2030.300, 2023.030). The judge pro tem's report and recommendation are adopted; plaintiff's motion to compel a further response to special interrogatory 13 is GRANTED. The response shall be a single narrative regarding efforts to comply with the discovery. The motion for sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Crown Building is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats the tentative ruling verbatim and attaches the report and recommendation and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JOE DICKINSON VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, May 20, 2019, line 11. DEFENDANT JOHN NEMEDEZ'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Defendant John Narito Nemendez's motion to quash service for lack of personal jurisdiction is DENIED. A party may contractually consent to personal jurisdiction in California, as was so here. (Bushansky v. Soon-Siong (2018) 23 Cal.App.5th 1000, 1008.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats the tentative ruling verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)
